author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter X. 11–13.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus is speaking to His sheep—to those already so, and to those yet to become such—who were then present; for in the place where they were, there were those who were already His sheep, as well as those who were afterwards to become so: and He likewise shows to those then present and those to come, both to them and to us, and to as many also after us as shall yet be His sheep, who it is that had been sent to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All, therefore, hear the voice of their Shepherd saying, “I am the good Shepherd.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would not add “good,” were there not bad shepherds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the bad shepherds are those who are thieves and robbers, or certainly hirelings at the best.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we ought to examine into, to distinguish, and to know, all the characters whom He has here depicted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord has already unfolded two points, which He had previously set forth in a kind of covert form: we already know that He is Himself the door, and we know that He is Himself the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who the thieves and robbers are, was made clear in yesterday’s lesson; and to-day we have heard of the hireling, as we have heard also of the wolf.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yesterday the porter was also introduced by name.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among the good, therefore, are the door, the doorkeeper, the shepherd, and the sheep: among the bad, the thieves and robbers, the hirelings, and the wolf.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We understand the Lord Christ as the door, and also as the Shepherd; but who is to be understood as the doorkeeper?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the former two, He has Himself explained: the doorkeeper He has left us to search out for ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what doth He say of the doorkeeper?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To him,” He saith, “the porter [doorkeeper] openeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom doth he open?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What doth he open to the Shepherd?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is also the door?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Shepherd Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, if Christ the Lord had not Himself explained, had not Himself said, “I am the Shepherd,” and “I am the door,” would any of us have ventured to say that Christ is Himself both the Shepherd and the door?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had He said, “I am the Shepherd,” and had not said, “I am the door,” we should be setting ourselves to inquire what was the door, and perhaps, mistaken in our views, be still standing before the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His grace and mercy have revealed to us the Shepherd, by His calling Himself so; have revealed to us also the door, when declared Himself such; but He hath left us to search out the doorkeeper for ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whom, then, are we to call the doorkeeper?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whomsoever we fix upon, we must take care not to think of him as greater than the door itself; for in men’s houses the doorkeeper is greater than the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The doorkeeper is placed before the door, not the door before the doorkeeper; because the porter keepeth the door, not the door the porter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I dare not say that any one is greater than the door, for I have heard already what is the door: that is no longer unknown to me, I am not left to my own conjecture, and I have not got much room for mere human guess work: God hath said it, the Truth hath said it, and we cannot change what the Unchangeable hath uttered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In respect, then, of the profound nature of this question, I shall tell you what I think: let each one make the choice that pleases him, but let him think of it reverently; as it is written, “Think of the Lord with goodness, and in simplicity of heart seek Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps we ought to understand the Lord Himself as the doorkeeper: for the shepherd and the door are in human respects as much different from each other as the doorkeeper and the door; and yet the Lord has called Himself both the Shepherd and the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, may we not understand Him also as the doorkeeper?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we look at His personal qualities, the Lord Christ is neither a shepherd, in the way we are accustomed to know and to see shepherds; nor is He a door, for no artisan made Him: but if, because of some point of similarity, He is both the door and the Shepherd, I venture to say, He is also a sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: True, the sheep is under the shepherd; yet He is both the Shepherd and a sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where is He the Shepherd?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look, here thou hast it; read the Gospel: “I am the good Shepherd.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where is He a sheep?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask the prophet: “He was led as a sheep to the slaughter.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask the friend of the bridegroom: “Behold the Lamb of God, that taketh away the sin of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, I am going to say something of a still more wonderful kind, in accordance with these points of similarity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For both the lamb, and the sheep, and the shepherd are friendly with one another, but from the lions as their foes the sheep are protected by their shepherds: and yet of Christ, who is both sheep and Shepherd, we have it said, “The Lion of the tribe of Judah hath prevailed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this, brethren, understand in connection with points of similarity, not with personal qualities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a common thing to see the shepherds sitting on a rock, and there guarding the cattle committed to their care.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely the shepherd is better than the rock that he sits upon; and yet Christ is both the Shepherd and the rock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this by way of comparison.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou askest me for His peculiar personal quality: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou askest me for the personal quality peculiarly His own: The only Son, from everlasting to everlasting begotten of the Father, the equal of Him that begat, the Maker of all things, unchangeable with the Father, unchanged by the assuming of human form, man by incarnation, the Son of man, and the Son of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this that I have said is not figure, but reality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, let us not, brethren, be disturbed in understanding Him, in harmony with certain resemblances, as Himself the door, and also the doorkeeper.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is the door?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The way of entrance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is the doorkeeper?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who opens it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who, then, is He that opens Himself, but He who unveils Himself to sight?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, when the Lord spoke at first of the door, we did not understand: so long as we did not understand, it was shut: He who opened it is Himself the doorkeeper.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is no need, then, of seeking any other meaning, no need; but perhaps there is the desire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If there is so, quit not the path, go not outside of the Trinity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou art in quest of some other impersonation of the doorkeeper, bethink thee of the Holy Spirit; for the Holy Spirit will not think it unmeet to be the doorkeeper, when the Son has thought it meet to be Himself the door.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look at the doorkeeper as perhaps the Holy Spirit: about Him the Lord saith to His disciples, “He shall guide you into all truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the door?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who, then, openeth the door, but He who guideth into all truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what are we to say of the hireling?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is not mentioned here among the good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The good Shepherd,” He says, “giveth His life for the sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he that is an hireling, and not the Shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth; and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The hireling does not here bear a good character, and yet in some respects is useful; nor would he be called an hireling, did he not receive hire from his employer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who then is this hireling, that is both blameworthy and needful?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And here, brethren, let the Lord Himself give us light, that we may know who the hirelings are, and be not hirelings ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who then is the hireling?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are some in office in the church, of whom the Apostle Paul saith, “Who seek their own, not the things that are Jesus Christ’s.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means that, “Who seek their own”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who do not love Christ freely, who do not seek after God for His own sake; who are pursuing after temporal advantages, gaping for gain, coveting honors from men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When such things are loved by an overseer, and for such things God is served, whoever such an one may be, he is an hireling who cannot count himself among the children.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of such also the Lord saith: “Verily, I say unto you, they have their reward.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to what the Apostle Paul says of St. Timothy: “But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your circumstances; for I have no man like-minded, who will naturally care for you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The shepherd mourned in the midst of hirelings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He sought some one who sincerely loved the flock of Christ, and round about him, amongst those who were with him at that time, he found not one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that there was no one then in the Church of Christ but the Apostle Paul and Timothy, who had a brother’s concern for the flock; but it so happened at the time of his sending Timothy, that he had none else of his sons about him; only hirelings were with him, “who sought their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet he himself, with a brother’s anxiety for the flock, preferred sending his son, and remaining himself amongst hirelings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hirelings are also found among ourselves, but the Lord alone distinguisheth them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that searcheth the heart, distinguisheth them; and yet sometimes we know them ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was not without a purpose that the Lord Himself said also of the wolves: “By their fruits ye shall know them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Temptations put many to the question, and then their thoughts are made manifest; but many remain undiscovered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord’s fold must have as overseers, both those who are children and those who are hirelings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the overseers, who are sons, are the shepherds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they are shepherds, how is there but one Shepherd, save that all of them are members of the one Shepherd, to whom the sheep belong?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they are also members of Himself as the one sheep; because “as a sheep he was led to the slaughter.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But give heed to the fact that even the hirelings are needful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For many indeed in the Church are following after earthly profit, and yet preach Christ, and through them is heard the voice of Christ; and the sheep follow, not the hireling, but the Shepherd’s voice speaking through the hireling.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearken to the hirelings as pointed out by the Lord Himself: “The scribes,” He saith, “and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ seat: do what they say; but do not what they do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What else said He but, Listen to the Shepherd’s voice speaking through the hirelings?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For sitting in Moses’ seat, they teach the law of God; therefore God teacheth by them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if they wish to teach their own things, hear them not, do them not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For certainly such seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s; but no hireling has dared to say to Christ’s people, Seek your own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For his own evil conduct he does not preach from the seat of Christ: he does injury by the evil that he does, not by the good that he says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pluck the grapes, beware of the thorn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is well I see that you have understood; but for the sake of those that are slower, I shall repeat these words with greater plainness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How said I, Pluck the bunch of grapes, beware of the thorn; when the Lord saith, “Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is quite true: and yet what I said is also true, Pluck the bunch of grapes, beware of the thorn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For sometimes the grape-cluster, springing from the root of the vine, finds its support in a common hedge; its branch, grows, becomes embedded among thorns, and the thorn bears other fruit than its own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the thorn has not been produced from the vine, but has become the resting-place of its runner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Make thine inquiries only at the roots.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Seek for the thorn-root, thou wilt find it apart from the vine: seek the origin of the grape, and from the root of the vine it will be found to have sprung.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, Moses’ seat was the vine; the morals of the Pharisees were the thorns.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sound doctrine cometh through the wicked, as the vine-branch in a hedge, a bunch of grapes among thorns.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Gather carefully, so as in seeking the fruit not to tear thine hand; and while thou art to hear one speaking what is good, imitate him not when doing what is evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “What they tell you, do,”—gather the grapes; “but what they do, do not,”—beware of the thorns.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even through hirelings listen to the voice of the Shepherd, but be not hirelings yourselves, seeing ye are members of the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, Paul himself, the holy apostle who said, “I have no one who hath a brother’s concern about you; for all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s,” draws a distinction in another place between hirelings and sons; and see what he saith: “Some preach Christ even of envy and strife, and some also of good will: some of love, knowing that I am set for the defence of the gospel; but some also preach Christ of contention, not sincerely, supposing to add affliction to my bonds.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These were hirelings who disliked the Apostle Paul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why such dislike, but just because they were seeking after temporal things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But mark what he adds: “What then, notwithstanding, every way, whether in pretence or in truth, Christ is preached: and I therein do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ is the truth: let the truth be preached in pretense by hirelings, let it be preached in truth by the children: the children are waiting patiently for the eternal inheritance of the Father, the hirelings are longing for, and in a hurry to get, the temporal pay of their employer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For my part let me be shorn of the human glory, which I see such an object of envy to hirelings: and yet by the tongues both of hirelings and of children let the divine glory of Christ be published abroad, seeing that, “whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is preached.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have seen who the hireling is also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who, but the devil, is the wolf?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what was said of the hireling?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When he seeth the wolf coming, he fleeth: but the sheep are not his own, and he careth not for the sheep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was the Apostle Paul such an one?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was Peter such an one?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far from it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was such the character of the other apostles, save Judas, the son of perdition?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were they shepherds then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly they were.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how is there one Shepherd?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have already said they were shepherds, because members of the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that head they rejoiced, under that head they were in harmony together, with one spirit they lived in the bond of one body; and therefore belonged all of them to the one Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, they were shepherds, and not hirelings, wherefore fled they when suffering persecution?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Explain it to us, O Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In an epistle, I have seen Paul fleeing: he was let down by the wall in a basket, to escape the hands of his persecutor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had he, then, no care of the sheep, whom he thus abandoned at the approach of the wolf?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Clearly he had, but he commended them by his prayers to the Shepherd who was sitting in heaven; and for their advantage he preserved himself by flight, as he says in a certain place, “To abide in the flesh is needful for you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all had heard from the Shepherd Himself, “If they persecute you in one city, flee ye into another.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May the Lord be pleased to explain to us this point!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lord, Thou saidst to those whom Thou didst certainly wish to be faithful shepherds, and whom Thou didst form into Thine own members, “If they persecute you, flee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Doest Thou, then, injustice to them, when Thou blamest the hirelings who flee when they see the wolf coming!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We ask Thee to tell us what meaning lies hid in the depths of the question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us knock, and the keeper of the door, which is Christ, will be here to reveal Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is the hireling that seeth the wolf coming, and fleeth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that seeketh his own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is one that does not venture plainly to rebuke an offender.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look, some one or other has sinned—grievously sinned; he ought to be rebuked, to be excommunicated: but once excommunicated, he will turn into an enemy, hatch plots, and do all the injury he can.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At present, he who seeketh his own, not the things that are Jesus Christ’s, in order not to lose what he follows after, the advantages of human friendship, and incur the annoyances of human enmity, keeps quiet and does not administer rebuke.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, the wolf has caught a sheep by the throat; the devil has enticed a believer into adultery: thou holdest thy peace—thou utterest no reproof.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O hireling, thou hast seen the wolf coming and hast fled!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps he answers and says: See, I am here; I have not fled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast fled, because thou hast been silent; thou hast been silent, because thou hast been afraid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flight of the mind is fear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou stoodest with thy body, thou fleddest in thy spirit, which was not the conduct of him who said, “Though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how did he flee in spirit, who, though absent in the flesh, yet in his letters reproved the fornicators?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our affections are the motions of our minds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Joy is expansion of the mind; sorrow, contraction of the mind; desire, a forward movement of the mind; and fear, the flight of the mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou art expanded in mind when thou art glad; contracted in mind when thou art in trouble; thou movest forward in mind when thou hast an earnest desire; and thou fleest in mind when thou art afraid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, is how the hireling is said to flee at the sight of the wolf.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because he careth not for the sheep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why “careth he not for the sheep”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because he is an hireling.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that, “he is an hireling”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He seeketh a temporal reward, and shall not dwell in the house for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are still some things here to be inquired about and discussed with you, but it is not prudent to burden you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are ministering the Lord’s food to our fellow-servants; we feed as sheep in the Lord’s pastures, and are fed together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And just as we must not withhold what is needful, so our weak hearts are not to be overcharged with the abundance of provisions.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it not then annoy your Charity that I do not take up to-day all that I think is still here to be discussed; but the same lesson will, in the Lord’s name, be read over to us again on the preaching days, and be, with His help, more carefully considered.
